name: Limb-a-tron

consumes.minimum: 1 active composition (no layers selected for creation mode; a Limb-a-tron controller or shape layer selected for bake mode)
consumes.description: Creation mode: reads the active CompItem for dimensions (width/height) to place the shape layer and controller null at comp centre. Bake mode: reads the selected layer(s) to locate the LimbControlsV3 pseudo-effect, then traverses the full parent-chain and all keyframes on the controller null and shape layer.

controls.name: Limb Name
controls.role: sets the name used for the shape layer, controller null, and pseudo-effect instance; also injected into every expression string at build time

controls.name: OK
controls.role: confirms limb name entry and triggers rig creation

controls.name: Cancel
controls.role: dismisses the name dialog without creating a rig

controls.name: Yes (bake confirm)
controls.role: confirms bake-and-remove-controller operation on a selected Limb-a-tron rig

controls.name: No (bake confirm)
controls.role: dismisses the bake confirmation dialog without baking

functions: guards when no active composition is present
guards when selected layers are not a Limb-a-tron rig
shows a name-input dialog for new rig creation
creates a named shape layer with Regular Limb and Noodle Limb vector groups
creates a controller null layer with a LimbControlsV3 pseudo effect applied from a temp FFX binary
wires IK-angle, adjusted-length, and pop-prevent expressions onto the controller null pseudo-effect sliders
wires elbow and wrist comp-space / layer-space position expressions onto the controller null
wires shape-path expressions (regular and noodle body) onto the shape layer vector groups
wires fill color and fill opacity expressions linked to the controller pseudo-effect
wires linked stroke-pass expressions (color, width, trim start/end/offset) for regular and noodle render passes
wraps all mutations in a named undo group
detects existing Limb-a-tron rigs from selected layers via pseudo-effect introspection
shows a bake-confirm dialog when a rig is selected
bakes animated body-path expressions to per-frame keyframes with Hermite simplification
freezes static appearance properties (fill color, stroke width, trim ranges) as static values
removes inactive render group (Regular or Noodle) after baking based on Noodle Mode state
optionally removes the controller null layer after a confirmed bake

scriptSim.inputs: Empty composition (1920×1080, 24 fps, 5 s). No layers selected. User types 'Arm' into the name edittext then clicks OK.

operations.target: Arm/ADBE Root Vectors Group/Regular Limb/ADBE Vectors Group/Path 1/ADBE Vector Shape
Arm/ADBE Root Vectors Group/Regular Limb/ADBE Vectors Group/ADBE Vector Graphic - Fill/ADBE Vector Fill Color
Arm/ADBE Root Vectors Group/Regular Limb/ADBE Vectors Group/ADBE Vector Graphic - Fill/ADBE Vector Fill Opacity
Arm/ADBE Root Vectors Group/Noodle Limb/ADBE Vectors Group/Path 1/ADBE Vector Shape
Arm/ADBE Root Vectors Group/Noodle Limb/ADBE Vectors Group/ADBE Vector Graphic - Fill/ADBE Vector Fill Color
Arm/ADBE Root Vectors Group/Noodle Limb/ADBE Vectors Group/ADBE Vector Graphic - Fill/ADBE Vector Fill Opacity
Arm CTRL/Effects/Arm Limb Control/Adjusted U/L Limb Lengths
Arm CTRL/Effects/Arm Limb Control/Proximity / Pop Prevent Factor

expressions.target: Arm/ADBE Root Vectors Group/Regular Limb/ADBE Vectors Group/Path 1/ADBE Vector Shape

sim.note: Sweeps the upper IK angle from -90° to +90° (simulating the CTRL null orbiting around the shoulder), showing how the unified regular limb body path changes shape as the arm bends.
Sweeps across the 5-second composition (119 frames at 24 fps) to show how IK angles evolve over time as the controller null is animated — output is a [upperAngle, lowerAngle] 2D vector.

expressions.target: Arm CTRL/Effects/Arm Limb Control/Proximity / Pop Prevent Factor

sim.note: Sweeps Pop Prevent slider from 0 (no anti-pop) to 100 (maximum anti-pop), showing how the pop-prevent factor damps near-full-extension snapping.

expressions.target: Arm CTRL/Effects/Arm Limb Control/Adjusted U/L Limb Lengths

sim.note: Sweeps Pop Prevent 0–100 to show how adjusted upper/lower limb lengths contract as the anti-pop factor softens the full-extension singularity.
Samples the elbow layer-space position over the composition duration, showing how the elbow point tracks the IK solution as the controller is animated.
Samples the wrist layer-space position over the composition duration, tracking the IK end-effector position.

expressions.target: Arm/ADBE Root Vectors Group/Regular Limb/ADBE Vectors Group/ADBE Vector Graphic - Fill/ADBE Vector Fill Color
expressions.expression: thisComp.layer('Arm CTRL').effect('Arm Limb Control')('Arm Fill Color')

sim.note: Reads Arm Fill Color from the controller pseudo-effect directly; result is constant unless the control is keyframed.

expressions.target: Arm/ADBE Root Vectors Group/Regular Limb/ADBE Vectors Group/ADBE Vector Graphic - Fill/ADBE Vector Fill Opacity
expressions.expression: thisComp.layer('Arm CTRL').effect('Arm Limb Control')('Arm Fill Opacity')

sim.note: Sweeps Arm Fill Opacity 0–100 to confirm the fill opacity passes through linearly from the controller slider.

notes: The script runs in two exclusive modes: creation mode (no layers selected) and bake mode (Limb-a-tron rig selected). The same dialog/entry point dispatches both.

The LimbControlsV3 pseudo effect is shipped as a compiled binary (base-64 in the JSX) written to a temp .ffx file and applied via applyPreset — this requires 'Allow Scripts to Write Files' in AE preferences.

All expression strings are built at rig-creation time with the limb name interpolated directly into the source, so every expression references its controller null and shape layer by the exact name chosen in the dialog.
Bake mode collects every keyframe time across the full parent chain (up to 4 levels), resamples to per-frame times, then uses Hermite simplification (shapeError threshold) to prune redundant keys.

The regularLimbExpression builds a dense sampled polyline with cap arcs, elbow arc, and side-curvature bell curves — vertex count is fixed at (CAP_STEPS+1)+SIDE_STEPS+ELBOW_STEPS+SIDE_STEPS+CAP_STEPS+SIDE_STEPS+ELBOW_STEPS+SIDE_STEPS to keep the closed path topologically stable across all poses.
Noodle mode and Regular mode are mutually exclusive: the active mode path expression returns a real shape while the inactive path returns an empty fixed-size zero path.
